mission.title: Install and Configure Kuberhealthy on Kubernetes
mission.description: Kuberhealthy is a Kubernetes operator that runs synthetic checks as pods, allowing you to continuously verify that your applications and clusters are functioning as expected. It integrates seamlessly with Prometheus for monitoring and provides a simple JSON status page for easy access to check results.

steps.title: Add the Kuberhealthy Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Kuberhealthy Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add kuberhealthy https://kuberhealthy.github.io/charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Kuberhealthy using Helm
steps.description:
Install Kuberhealthy in the `kuberhealthy` namespace (it will be created if it doesn't exist) with the following command:
```bash
helm install kuberhealthy kuberhealthy/kuberhealthy --namespace kuberhealthy --create-namespace --version v2.7.1
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Kuberhealthy pods are running successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace kuberhealthy
```

steps.title: Port-forward to access the Kuberhealthy status page
steps.description:
Set up port forwarding to access the Kuberhealthy status page locally:
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/kuberhealthy --namespace kuberhealthy 8080:80
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits for Kuberhealthy
steps.description:
To ensure Kuberhealthy has appropriate resource limits, edit the deployment:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment kuberhealthy --namespace kuberhealthy
```
Add the following under `spec.template.spec.containers.resources`:
```yaml
resources:
  limits:
    cpu: "500m"
    memory: "512Mi"
  requests:
    cpu: "250m"
    memory: "256Mi"
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Kuberhealthy will show the Kuberhealthy pods running in the `kuberhealthy` namespace, and you will be able to access the Kuberhealthy status page at `http://localhost:8080`. Additionally, the resource limits will be set as specified.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add kuberhealthy https://kuberhealthy.github.io/charts
helm repo update
helm install kuberhealthy kuberhealthy/kuberhealthy --namespace kuberhealthy --create-namespace --version v2.7.1

prerequisites.description: You need a running Kubernetes cluster and the Helm and kubectl command-line tools installed.
